blockers.title: Admin event check-in can grant attendance without revalidating the member's current lane or lifecycle state
blockers.evidence: convex/admin/events.ts:740-818 checks the registration and event state, then patches the row to attended/no_show and, on attended, writes ActivityLog and calls maybePromoteStanding. It never loads reg.member_id to confirm the target member is still active and still allowed to attend that event audience. A suspended member, or a member later corrected to minor/restricted_unknown, can therefore be marked attended from a stale registration/pass. This violates the Stage 0 server-side role/restriction model, the suspended-access pause rule, the under-18 adult-event block, and spec A's event safeguarding claims.
blockers.required_fix: Before any attended/no_show write, fetch the registrant and require lifecycle_state === active plus laneSeesEvent(member.member_lane, event.audience_lane). Do not create attendance activity or standing promotion for ineligible members. Add tests for suspended, minor, and restricted_unknown registrants with stale registrations or passes.

blockers.title: Member profile updates accept unbounded free-text that is later exposed in member and admin surfaces
blockers.evidence: convex/members.ts:156-235 accepts profile fields and stores them after validateProfileFields. convex/lib/profile.ts:180-213 validates picklists and arrays, but does not cap or normalize headline, bio, current_job_title, current_employer, certifications_other, field_of_study, institution, or free-form role values allowed by roleValidForArea at convex/lib/profile.ts:164-171. These values are later surfaced in directory/admin/application views, including directory fields required by spec D. This is a system-boundary validation bypass.
blockers.required_fix: Add server-side trim, length limits, and array item limits for every member-submitted free-text field before storage. Reject or normalize over-limit values in Convex mutations, and add direct mutation tests proving oversized fields cannot be stored or displayed.

blockers.title: Immutable audit summaries store raw admin-entered reasons
blockers.evidence: convex/admin/opportunities.ts:425-433 writes closeOpportunity reason text into after_summary. convex/admin/certificates.ts:119-129 writes revokeCertificate reason text into after_summary. convex/admin/members.ts:600-611 writes status-change reason text into after_summary. convex/admin/audit.ts:23-50 then exposes after_summary to admins through the audit log. Free-form reasons commonly contain member-sensitive context, so the audit trail can become an immutable broad-read data leak instead of a minimal evidence trail.
blockers.required_fix: Do not store raw reason text in AuditLog summaries. Store operational reasons only on the appropriate domain record or role-limited note, and audit only structured facts such as reason_present, reason_code, or length bucket. Add regression tests asserting supplied reason text is absent from audit summaries.

non_blocking_notes: No member-facing em dash violations were found in added source strings; em/en dashes appeared only in README/SOURCE-MAP/comment or test-comment contexts.

Youth event empty-state copy is forward-looking while server-side publishEvent refuses youth events at launch. It is not the primary blocker because the server denies publication, but the copy should be aligned with the launch-phase youth signpost before production.
Full build and E2E verification were not run after the static review because the verdict is already DO-NOT-SHIP on code-level blockers.

spec_compliance.criterion: A. Events access, youth lane handling, priority windows, waitlist promotion, and check-in
spec_compliance.evidence: Most list/RSVP paths re-check active lifecycle and lane, but admin check-in does not revalidate the registrant before granting attendance and standing-impacting activity.

spec_compliance.criterion: B. Opportunities visibility, application gating, one-application rule, and result handling
spec_compliance.evidence: Reviewed opportunity list/get/apply/result paths gate by lifecycle and lane, exclude allies from women_only rows, enforce one application, and re-check winner eligibility.
Standing promotion can be reached through the unsafe check-in path because attended activity is recorded for a target member without current lifecycle/lane eligibility.
Directory listing requires directory_visible, active lifecycle, active_member+ standing, and standard/ally lane, and returned fields exclude email, mobile, gender, DOB, and bio.
Reviewed RSVP, waitlist promotion, event cancellation/postponement, application receipt/result, certificate issue, and standing-change notification paths.

spec_compliance.criterion: F. Admin members, certificates, contact reveal, and audit trail
spec_compliance.evidence: Contact reveal and super-admin certificate controls are present, but raw admin reasons are written into immutable audit summaries and exposed via admin audit log.
Partner admin mutations are admin-gated and validate key public fields such as website/logo inputs.

spec_compliance.criterion: H. Overview, reports, and platform health
spec_compliance.evidence: Admin overview/report paths are admin-gated, and platform health is restricted to super-admin.

spec_compliance.criterion: I. Portal shell, design, and brand locks
spec_compliance.evidence: No member-facing em dash strings were found in the added diff during review.
The slice cannot pass Gate 4 while the authorization, validation, and audit-trail blockers above remain untested and unfixed.

summary: DO-NOT-SHIP. The slice has a safeguarding-critical event check-in authorization gap, unbounded member-submitted profile text at a system boundary, and raw free-text leakage through immutable audit summaries.
